normalization.note: Prospect chooses weekly or monthly once. Mirror consistently. Convert internally.

universal_input_rule: Before any channel-specific ROI math, Bella must get: (1) ACV, (2) channel volume, (3) current channel conversions OR conversion rate, (4) agent-specific variable. DO NOT calculate uplift from channel volume alone.

Alex.primary_claim: Responding in under 1 minute can increase conversions by 391% versus much slower response times.
supporting: Under 5 minutes = 21x more likely to qualify.
Under 5 minutes = 100x more likely to connect.

uplift_tiers.already_near_instant: Position as reinforcement and consistency — not major uplift

Chris.primary_claim: Websites with AI chatbots see 23% higher conversion rates.
Chris.upside_only_claim: Engaged visitors can convert 4x more often.
Chris.rule: Use 23% as base case ALWAYS. Use 4x only as upside framing, NEVER default base-case math.

Maddie.primary_claim: 78% of customers buy from the first business that responds.

Sarah.primary_claim: Database reactivation campaigns can see around a 5% conversion rate from dormant leads to active deals.

James.primary_claim: A 1-star rating increase can drive about a 9% revenue boost.

use_when: any lead source where response speed matters
guardrails: Calculate channel by channel — do not blend channels without explaining it.
If follow-up already near-instant, position as reinforcement not huge uplift.
Ads AND website can both apply Alex, but explain separately.

use_when: no strong live conversion layer on page
guardrails: Use 23% uplift as conservative base model.
Use 4x only as upside framing, never base-case.
Chris is especially strong when ads go to a landing page with no live engagement.

use_when: missed calls or after-hours handling is weak
formula: Else if total_calls and poor coverage known: estimate recoverable_calls conservatively
guardrails: Do not claim exact missed-call rates unless provided.
Use 78% stat as commercial justification framing, not precise math driver.
If inputs weak, present as directional. Still recommend Maddie if phone dependence + weak after-hours is clear.
Preferred questions: how many inbound calls/week, what happens after hours, how many go unanswered, what % convert.

use_when: old lead count is known or credibly confirmed
guardrails: Use conservative dormant lead estimates when rough.
Even rough old-lead count is enough to calculate.

use_when: rating or reputation is a lever
formula: Apply 9% uplift as directional or modelled depending on confidence in time horizon
guardrails: Usually directional unless revenue baseline is strong.
Do not imply a guaranteed 1-star improvement timeline.
Frame James as a strategic revenue lever, not always a precise weekly cash calculator.

recommendation_logic.voice_bias: Recommend at least 2 voice agents when evidence supports it.
priority_rules: Ads running -> Alex + Chris become highly likely
Strong phone + no clear after-hours handling -> Chris + Maddie highly likely
Large old-lead database -> Sarah highly likely
Weak or no review ask system -> James relevant

output_policy.directional_language: I'd treat that one as directional rather than exact until we've got a little more data.
